name: OpenLayers (Open Source Web Mapping)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 2-Clause); OpenLayers team; no proprietary tile requirements

auth.notes: No auth for library; tile providers may require API keys

performance.notes: WebGL rendering for vector tiles and features; Canvas for raster; fast tile loading

agent_readiness.notes: Feature-rich, fully open-source web mapping library. No proprietary dependencies (works without Mapbox/Google). Supports: WMS, WMTS, WFS, GeoTIFF, Vector tiles, GeoJSON, KML, GPX, TopoJSON. WebGL vector rendering. Projections support (EPSG:4326, 3857, custom via proj4). OGC standards compliant. GIS application development with drawing tools, feature interactions. Mature, stable, 15+ years. Used in government, enterprise GIS, and OSM-based applications.

gotchas: Steeper learning curve than Leaflet or MapLibre
Verbose API for simple use cases
Documentation less polished than commercial alternatives
Bundle size is larger (modular imports help)
best_when: GIS applications requiring OGC standards (WMS, WFS, WMTS)
Applications needing no proprietary map service dependency
Government and enterprise GIS with custom data sources
Complex map interactions: drawing, editing, measuring
avoid_when: Simple web maps with markers (use Leaflet — simpler)
Vector tile styling at Mapbox GL quality level
summary: OpenLayers is a mature, fully open-source web mapping library with no proprietary dependencies. Supports OGC standards (WMS, WFS, WMTS), GeoTIFF, vector tiles, GeoJSON, KML. WebGL vector rendering. Custom projections via proj4. 15+ year history, used in enterprise GIS and government applications. More complex than Leaflet but much more capable for professional GIS applications requiring OGC compliance.
